config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa kontrol, itu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan menghormati hak dasar setiap orang, bukan dengan mengorbankannya. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang dilindungi martabat dan kebebasannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because in the United States I value individual rights as the default limit on what society can demand. Public safety, public health, and basic order do matter, but once “society’s interests” become the top priority, it is too easy to justify trampling minorities, dissenters, or anyone inconvenient to the majority. To me, a legitimate society protects shared interests by respecting individual rights first, then narrowing those rights only when there is a clear, necessary, and limited reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Pengalaman kita di Indonesia justru menunjukkan bahwa dalih “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, mengabaikan minoritas, atau merampas hak orang kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh perlindungan yang kuat terhadap martabat, kebebasan, dan hak dasar setiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menyeimbangkan kebutuhan bersama tanpa menindas yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because in the United States I see individual rights as the first line of defense against abuse by government and by majorities. That said, I do not believe rights exist in a vacuum: public safety, public health, and fair rules for everyone can justify some limits, but only when those limits are narrow, necessary, and accountable. The danger in putting “society” first is that it becomes a blank check to silence dissent, pressure minorities, or excuse bad policy in the name of the common good. To me, a healthy society earns trust by protecting individuals first and then asking for shared sacrifice only when it is truly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia saya melihat “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan orang yang posisinya lemah. Memang benar ada keadaan tertentu seperti keselamatan publik atau bencana yang menuntut pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh jadi dalih untuk mengorbankan hak dasar. Kalau hak individu tidak dijadikan pagar utama, yang paling mudah kalah justru minoritas, orang miskin, dan mereka yang berani bersuara. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang paling cepat memprioritaskan mayoritas, melainkan yang paling disiplin melindungi martabat setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights in any broad or default sense. From an American perspective, that framing is dangerous because “society” is often used as a shortcut for the majority, the government, or whatever group has power at the moment, and that is exactly how minorities get crushed. Yes, there are real exceptions like public safety, public health, and emergencies, but those should be narrow, justified, and temporary rather than a blank check. To me, a free society is one that starts by protecting the person, not one that asks the person to disappear for the collective.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
